title: Threat Intelligence Integrations Overview
content:
## What is Threat Intelligence?

Threat Intelligence (TI) encompasses the collection, processing, analysis, and dissemination of information about current and potential cyber threats. Threat intelligence platforms and feeds provide indicators of compromise (IOCs), adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs), vulnerability intelligence, and strategic threat landscape assessments. These tools enable security teams to proactively identify threats, enrich security alerts with contextual information, and make informed decisions about defensive priorities. Threat intelligence is the connective tissue that makes every other security tool more effective.

## Why Integrate Threat Intelligence with T1 Agentics?

Threat intelligence multiplies the value of every other integration in T1 Agentics. By connecting TI sources, you enable:

- **Automated Alert Enrichment**: Every alert from your EDR, SIEM, or network tools is automatically enriched with reputation scores, threat actor attribution, and related IOCs
- **AI-Powered Contextualization**: T1 Agentics' AI engine uses threat intelligence context to make smarter triage decisions, distinguishing between commodity malware and targeted APT activity
- **Proactive Threat Hunting**: Push newly published IOCs from threat feeds into your EDR and SIEM for retrospective and real-time hunting
- **Multi-Source Correlation**: Aggregate intelligence from multiple vendors and open-source feeds to reduce false positives and increase confidence in threat assessments
- **IOC Lifecycle Management**: Automatically expire, update, and manage IOCs across all your security tools from a single platform
- **Strategic Intelligence Distribution**: Share curated intelligence reports with stakeholders across the organization

## Available Integrations

T1 Agentics supports an extensive library of threat intelligence integrations spanning commercial platforms, open-source tools, lookup services, and data enrichment APIs. Key integrations include:

### Commercial Threat Intelligence Platforms

| Integration | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Recorded Future** | AI-powered threat intelligence with real-time risk scoring, dark web monitoring, and predictive analytics |
| **Mandiant Threat Intelligence** | Frontline intelligence from incident response engagements with adversary profiles and campaign tracking |
| **CrowdStrike Falcon Intel** | Adversary-focused intelligence with actor profiles, malware analysis, and strategic reports |
| **Anomali ThreatStream** | Threat intelligence platform with automated IOC management, scoring, and distribution |
| **Intel 471** | Underground intelligence focused on cybercriminal actors, malware, and vulnerability exploitation |
| **Kaspersky Threat Intelligence** | Multi-layered threat data including APT tracking, crimeware intelligence, and data feeds |
| **Cyble Vision** | Dark web and surface web intelligence with brand monitoring and vulnerability intelligence |
| **Silobreaker** | Threat intelligence and risk monitoring with automated report generation and trend analysis |
| **SOCRadar** | Extended threat intelligence with digital risk protection, attack surface management, and dark web monitoring |
| **ThreatConnect** | Intelligence-driven security operations platform with analytics, orchestration, and collaboration |
| **ThreatQuotient** | Threat intelligence platform with collaborative analysis, scoring, and integration framework |
| **Digital Shadows** | Digital risk protection with dark web monitoring, data leak detection, and brand protection |

### Open-Source Intelligence Platforms

| Integration | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **MISP** | Open-source threat intelligence sharing platform with extensive taxonomy and galaxy support |
| **OpenCTI** | Open-source cyber threat intelligence platform built on STIX2 with knowledge graph visualization |
| **AlienVault OTX** | Open threat exchange with community-contributed pulses and IOC feeds |
| **Maltego** | Visual link analysis tool for investigating relationships between entities |
| **SpiderFoot** | Open-source OSINT automation for reconnaissance and threat intelligence gathering |

### IOC Lookup and Reputation Services

| Integration | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **VirusTotal** | Multi-engine malware scanning and file/URL/IP reputation with community intelligence |
| **Shodan** | Internet-connected device search engine for discovering exposed services and vulnerabilities |
| **GreyNoise** | Internet background noise analysis distinguishing targeted attacks from mass scanning |
| **AbuseIPDB** | Community-driven IP address abuse reporting and lookup database |
| **Hybrid Analysis** | Free malware analysis service with CrowdStrike Falcon Sandbox backend |
| **Pulsedive** | Community threat intelligence with IOC enrichment, risk scoring, and feed management |
| **BinaryEdge** | Internet-wide scanning data for attack surface discovery and threat intelligence |
| **ONYPHE** | Cyber defense search engine for internet-connected assets and threat intelligence |
| **SecurityTrails** | Historical DNS and domain intelligence for investigating infrastructure changes |
| **WhoisXML API** | Domain, IP, and DNS intelligence with historical WHOIS and passive DNS data |
| **Hunter.io** | Email address discovery and verification for investigating phishing campaigns |
| **Have I Been Pwned** | Breached credential database for checking if accounts have been compromised |
| **IPInfo** | IP geolocation, ASN, and hosting detection for enriching network-based IOCs |
| **MaxMind** | IP geolocation and risk scoring database for network intelligence |
| **MXToolbox** | Email and DNS diagnostic tools for investigating mail-based threats |
| **URLScan.io** | Website scanning service for analyzing suspicious URLs and web-based threats |
| **PhishTank** | Community-driven phishing URL verification database |

### Additional Data Sources and APIs

| Integration | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **DomainTools Iris Investigate** | Domain and DNS intelligence with risk scoring and connected infrastructure mapping |
| **RiskIQ / Defender TI** | Microsoft's threat intelligence with internet data sets, host pairs, and component analysis |
| **Cofense Intelligence** | Phishing-specific threat intelligence from human-reported phishing emails |
| **Cofense Triage** | Phishing email analysis and triage platform for reported suspicious emails |
| **NIST NVD** | National Vulnerability Database with CVE details, CVSS scores, and CPE data |
| **IBM X-Force Exchange** | Threat intelligence sharing platform with IP/URL/malware reputation data |
| **Cisco Talos Intelligence** | Threat intelligence from Cisco's research team with reputation lookups and vulnerability data |
| **Cisco Umbrella Investigate** | DNS-layer threat intelligence with domain risk scoring and related infrastructure |
| **ReversingLabs** | Advanced file reputation and malware analysis with explainable threat intelligence |
| **DNSDB** | Passive DNS database for historical DNS resolution data |
| **Censys** | Internet-wide scanning and search for discovering assets and vulnerabilities |
| **HYAS Insight** | Adversary infrastructure intelligence with attribution and predictive analysis |
| **HYAS Protect** | Protective DNS with real-time threat intelligence for blocking malicious domains |
| **Red Canary** | Managed detection with curated threat intelligence and validated detections |
| **Group-IB Threat Intelligence** | Adversary-centric intelligence with dark web monitoring and brand protection |
| **ZeroFox** | Digital risk protection with social media, dark web, and surface web intelligence |
| **Mnemonic** | Passive DNS and threat intelligence services from Nordic security researchers |
| **DeepSight** | Symantec's threat intelligence with vulnerability, malware, and reputation data |
| **Silent Push** | Threat intelligence focused on attacker infrastructure with predictive indicators |
| **Sekoia** | European threat intelligence and XDR platform with CTI-driven detection |
| **Intezer** | Genetic malware analysis using code similarity for attribution and classification |

This category also includes numerous data connectors for databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite, BigQuery, AWS DynamoDB), collaboration tools (Confluence, GitHub, Slack), and utility services that support threat intelligence workflows.

## Key Capabilities

When you connect threat intelligence tools to T1 Agentics, the following capabilities become available:

- **IOC Enrichment**: Automatically look up file hashes, IP addresses, domains, and URLs against multiple TI sources when alerts are ingested
- **Reputation Scoring**: Aggregate reputation scores from multiple vendors to calculate a consolidated risk score for any indicator
- **Threat Actor Attribution**: Map observed TTPs and infrastructure to known threat actors and campaigns
- **MITRE ATT&CK Mapping**: Enrich detections with MITRE ATT&CK technique and tactic references from threat intelligence context
- **IOC Distribution**: Push validated IOCs to EDR, SIEM, firewall, and proxy tools for blocking and detection
- **Feed Management**: Ingest, deduplicate, and manage multiple threat intelligence feeds with automatic aging and confidence scoring
- **Dark Web Monitoring**: Receive alerts when your organization's assets, credentials, or data appear on dark web forums and marketplaces
- **Vulnerability Intelligence**: Cross-reference CVEs with exploit availability and active exploitation data from TI sources

## Getting Started

To connect a threat intelligence integration in T1 Agentics:

1. Navigate to **Connect** from the left sidebar
2. Click **Marketplace** to open the integration catalog
3. Use the category filter to select **Threat Intelligence** or search by vendor name
4. Click **Configure** on your desired threat intelligence integration
5. Enter the required API keys, tokens, or credentials as specified in the individual integration guide
6. Click **Test Connection** to verify connectivity
7. Configure enrichment preferences including which IOC types to look up and caching policies

## Best Practices

- **Layer your intelligence**: Use a combination of commercial platforms (depth), open-source feeds (breadth), and lookup services (speed) for comprehensive coverage
- **Set confidence thresholds**: Not all threat intelligence is equal. Configure minimum confidence scores for IOCs before they trigger automated blocking actions
- **Enable caching**: IOC lookups can be API-intensive. Enable caching for reputation lookups to reduce API consumption and speed up enrichment
- **Age out stale IOCs**: Indicators have a shelf life. Configure automatic expiration policies to prevent false positives from outdated IOCs
- **Prioritize enrichment sources**: Order your TI integrations by reliability and speed so the most valuable enrichment sources are queried first
- **Monitor API quotas**: Commercial TI APIs often have daily query limits. Monitor usage and configure rate limiting to avoid exhausting quotas during high-alert periods
- **Integrate with MISP or OpenCTI**: Use an open-source TIP as a central intelligence hub that aggregates feeds before distributing to T1 Agentics
- **Track intelligence effectiveness**: Measure which TI sources contribute most to true positive detections and optimize your subscription investments accordingly

## Related Playbooks

The following playbook templates work well with threat intelligence integrations:

- **IOC Enrichment Workflow**: Multi-source indicator enrichment triggered by any new alert, querying all connected TI sources in parallel
- **Phishing URL Analysis**: Automated URL reputation checking, screenshot capture, and sandbox detonation for suspected phishing links
- **Malware Hash Investigation**: File hash lookup across VirusTotal, malware sandboxes, and threat intelligence platforms with automated classification
- **Threat Actor Tracking**: Monitor specific threat actors across intelligence platforms and proactively hunt for their known IOCs in your environment
- **IOC Sweep and Block**: When high-confidence IOCs are published, automatically distribute them to all blocking and detection tools
- **Vulnerability Exploitation Monitoring**: Track CVEs with active exploitation intelligence and cross-reference against your vulnerability scan data
- **Dark Web Alert Response**: Automated investigation and notification workflow when organizational data is discovered on dark web sources
